finding.contract_claim: Parameter `error` is declared in the signature of log(message, error=False) (src/app/launcher.py line 1) as an accepted input that callers may pass.
finding.observed_behavior: The attributed call site, log("ok") in src/app/cli.py, does not pass `error`, so the parameter appears to always take its default False. `log` is a common name with high fan-out: src/noise.py defines an unrelated log(message) that is called locally.
finding.triage_reasoning: Reality: The reference sweep covers 3 files and finds exactly 1 match for `error` — inside the function definition itself (same file, quoted string). The only identified external call site `log("ok")` in `src/app/cli.py` does not pass `error`. No counter-evidence of any caller supplying a real value exists within the 3-file sweep scope. The function body at L2 (`return f"{message}:{error}"`) always uses the default `False`. Significance: The parameter advertises a supported flag that is never exercised; removing it eliminates dead API surface. Actionability: Remove the `error` parameter from the signature and simplify the f-string to `return f"{message}"`.
finding.suggested_fix: Remove the `error` parameter from `log(message, error=False)` and update the body to `return f"{message}"` (or `return message`). If the flag was intended for future use, document it as such rather than leaving a silently dead parameter.
